transcript:
Discovery Call — Irvine Legal
Date: May 29, 2026
Sales Rep: Dan Bryant
Prospect: Joshua Irvine

FIRM OVERVIEW
Firm: Irvine Legal
Attorney: Joshua Irvine (solo attorney)
Location: Davis County, Utah (one county north of Salt Lake City). Serves all of Utah; also licensed in Arizona but focused on Utah expansion first.
Practice: Construction-focused business law. Primary service is subscription-based "outsourced general counsel" for construction companies. Also handles litigation (active 20–25 litigation cases).
Staff: 8–9 total (4–5 legal assistants, 4–5 paralegals). Has a dedicated sales team that handles consultations independently. Joshua only gets involved for litigation matters.
Registered Agent: Serves as registered agent for ~800 clients, renewing entities annually — a manual, repetitive process he wants to automate.
Website: Has 600+ blog posts/articles built over ~5 years, including some podcast-driven content.

FINANCIALS
Current Revenue: ~$65,000/month (~$780K/year)
Goal — End of 2026: $100,000/month run rate (needs ~100 additional new clients beyond the ~60 he expects to close organically)
Long-Term Goal: $5M–$8M/year with $3M total owner benefit
Business Model: Subscription/retainer-based general counsel for construction businesses. Flat-fee and retainer model. Litigation billed separately.

MARKETING & LEAD GENERATION
Current Source: 100% referrals. No consistent digital marketing.
Past Attempts: Small Google Ads tests (~$1,000), Legal Match — minimal results due to lack of strategy and follow-through.
Content: 600+ blog posts, some immigration-related content from old practice area (no longer relevant). Dan noted technical SEO optimization is needed.
Target Geography: Salt Lake City metro (Davis, Salt Lake, Utah, Weber counties — roughly 80% of state population). Park City (east) is premium/wealthy. Plans to expand statewide and eventually into Arizona.
Ad Strategy Discussed: Lead with Google PPC (~$3,000/month ad spend) for high-intent searches (construction attorneys, business law Utah). Facebook/Instagram video ads (~$1,200–$1,500/month) for brand awareness and remarketing. LSA tested but PPC preferred — LSA costs ~$120/lead vs ~$60 for PPC in this market. Competitor reference: Amber (Georgia business law client) gets ~$60/lead on PPC.
Organic Strategy: Website redesign (WordPress), full SEO optimization for Utah construction/business law keywords, Google Maps ranking.

INTAKE & SALES
No formal intake process currently — receptionist answers and routes to sales team.
Sales team handles all consultations and closes subscription clients independently.
Joshua only handles litigation consultations/closes.
Conversion: Very high currently (all referral). Will need structured intake process when ads launch.
SMB Team resource offered: Intake & Sales University scripts/training.

TEAM & OPERATIONS
Biggest hiring challenge: Identifying A-players before hiring; traditional interviews/resumes no longer reliable filter.
Wants to hire a litigation attorney — has candidates in mind to poach once revenue justifies $200K+ offer.
Firm can run ~1–2 weeks without Joshua before things break (contract reviews, litigation deadlines).
Joshua handles: final review of all outgoing contracts/documents, all litigation.

AI & TECHNOLOGY
Strong interest in AI automation. Specifically wants:
  1. Automate annual registered agent entity renewals (800 clients, ~3–5 min each)
  2. General workflow streamlining
Discussed: SMB Team Fractional CTO service ($3,297/month bundled). Joshua interested but sees it as a phase-two priority.
Also mentioned wanting help setting up Claude AI skills/workflows for the firm.

PROPOSAL OPTIONS DISCUSSED
Option A — Full Service Marketing Starter only: ~$5,697/month + ad spend (~$4,500) = ~$10,200/month total
Option B — Elite 360 Max Starter (Marketing + Elite Coach): ~$7,400/month + ad spend = ~$11,900/month total
Option C — Phase approach: Start with marketing only, add coaching and CTO in later phases
Dan's recommendation: Start with Full Service Marketing Starter + ad spend. Website redesign takes ~90 days to go live. PPC and social ads launch sooner for near-term leads. Coaching can be layered in.
Joshua's posture: Transparent, analytical, not price-sensitive if deliverables match expectations. Said he was "probably going to end up going with you guys." Wants to review proposal before Tuesday meeting.

PERSONAL / RAPPORT
Family: 4 boys (20, 18 — just graduated high school, 9th grade, elementary). Oldest in college studying bioinformatics. Second son starting welding career at $80K/year out of high school.
Personal goal: 6-week Himalaya hiking trip when firm is self-sustaining.
Non-profit work: "Education Elevated" — mentoring fathers/families in construction industry. Also working on women's education initiative for developing countries.
Values: Community impact, transparency, no ego. Financially comfortable but not motivated purely by money — wants firm growth to fund nonprofit work.
Obstacle: Young kids limit time freedom currently despite financial means.
